section_label: NICU batch 4 — supportive, antimicrobials, neurology, NAS, hypertension, antifibrinolytic
review_disclaimer: DRAFT — pending clinician review. Verify every dose against your local protocol and current vial concentration before administration.

doses.max_per_day_all_routes: 3 g/kg (= 60 mL/kg) in the first hour or until perfusion improves / hepatomegaly develops

doses.frequency: Single dose ~ 1 hr before exchange transfusion

drugs.monitoring: BP, HR, SpO₂, perfusion; serum albumin; signs of fluid overload.
drugs.adverse_effects: Volume overload, mild allergic reactions (rare).
drugs.contraindications: Severe anaemia / heart failure (without prior transfusion).

review.notes: Crystalloid (NS) is preferred first-line over albumin for routine volume resuscitation. Consider albumin in shock with low oncotic pressure.

doses.indication: Restoration of central venous catheter patency (occlusion)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 1 mg/mL — instil 110% of internal lumen volume (max 2 mg in 2 mL); a lower 0.5 mg dose has been used in infants ≤ 10 kg

doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_range: Up to 0.5 mg/kg/hour catheter-directed; reduce to 0.1 mg/kg/hr if local bleeding (stop 1 h, restart lower)
doses.comments: Discontinue heparin several hours before starting. PT, aPTT, fibrinogen, platelets at baseline + every 4–6 h.

drugs.monitoring: PT, aPTT, fibrinogen (keep > 1 g/L), platelets (keep > 50k), bleeding signs, cranial USG (preterm).
drugs.adverse_effects: Major bleeding (esp. intracranial, GI), hypotension, anaphylaxis (rare).
drugs.contraindications: Active major bleeding, recent surgery / trauma / IVH, severe HTN, GA < 32 weeks (relative — IVH risk), known hypersensitivity.

review.notes: Alteplase use in extreme preterm requires urgent multidisciplinary review (neonatology, paediatric haematology, IR).

doses.indication: Mild bacterial infection / completion oral therapy
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Manufacturer max 30 mg/kg/day divided every 12 h

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response.
drugs.adverse_effects: Rash, diarrhoea, candidiasis.

review.notes: Oral amoxicillin is used for outpatient completion of treatment after IV ampicillin.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Use lowest effective dose / concentration. Specific technique-driven doses; consult an anaesthetist.
doses.max_per_dose: 2 mg/kg single dose (continuous infusion typical max 0.2–0.4 mg/kg/hr in neonates)

drugs.monitoring: ECG, BP, neurologic status; methaemoglobin level if cyanosis develops.
drugs.adverse_effects: Cardiotoxicity (worst with rapid IV exposure), seizure, methaemoglobinaemia, respiratory depression with high spinal block.

review.notes: Use only by trained anaesthetist. Not a first-line pain modality on the NICU floor.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 20–80 mg/kg/day elemental Ca, in 2–4 divided doses
Start 20 mg/kg/day elemental Ca + 10–20 mg/kg/day elemental P; titrate to max 70–80 mg/kg/day Ca + 40–50 mg/kg/day P
doses.comments: Always pair Ca with P supplementation in osteopenia.

drugs.monitoring: Serum Ca, P, alk-phos, urine Ca/Cr ratio.
drugs.adverse_effects: Constipation, mild GI upset, milk-alkali syndrome (high dose).

review.notes: Different oral salts have very different elemental Ca per mL — always document mg of elemental Ca, not mL or salt.

drugs.category: Urea cycle drug — N-acetylglutamate synthase analog

doses.indication: Acute hyperammonaemia (NAGS deficiency, propionic acidemia, methylmalonic acidemia)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 100–250 mg/kg/day in 2–4 divided doses (round to nearest 100 mg)
doses.comments: Initiate as soon as diagnosis suspected. Titrate to maintain plasma ammonia in normal range. Co-administer with other ammonia-lowering therapies (alternate pathway drugs, dialysis, protein restriction).

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Long-term doses individualised by metabolic specialist; typical 10–100 mg/kg/day divided

drugs.monitoring: Plasma ammonia, urea cycle metabolites, weight, growth, neurology.
drugs.adverse_effects: Vomiting, diarrhoea, abdominal pain.

review.notes: Specialty drug — initiate only with metabolic medicine team.

doses.frequency: Single dose; may repeat once after 30 min if inadequate

drugs.monitoring: RR, SpO₂, HR continuously during and after sedation; airway patency.
drugs.adverse_effects: Long unpredictable half-life; respiratory depression, hypotension, GI upset, paradoxical excitation. Reports of sudden death — falling out of favour.
drugs.contraindications: Hepatic / renal impairment, GI ulceration, hypersensitivity.

review.notes: Oral / rectal product no longer manufactured in some countries. Long half-life and sudden death reports — POOR CHOICE for routine procedural sedation in 2024+.

doses.indication: Anaemia of prematurity (limited evidence — not routinely recommended)
doses.comments: Always co-administer iron 2–6 mg/kg/day. Most meta-analyses show no clinically significant transfusion reduction.

drugs.monitoring: Hb, reticulocytes, iron status, BP.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypertension, polycythaemia, pure red cell aplasia (rare).

review.notes: Routine use NOT recommended — neither early nor late. May be considered for Jehovah's Witness families or specific situations.

doses.indication: Pertussis (only if azithromycin unavailable / aged > 1 mo)
Severe infection — IV (when oral not possible)

drugs.monitoring: Watch for hypertrophic pyloric stenosis (especially under 6 weeks); QT.
drugs.adverse_effects: HYPERTROPHIC PYLORIC STENOSIS (KIDs list — high risk in neonates < 6 weeks), QT prolongation, GI cramping.

review.notes: KIDs list — avoid in neonates UNLESS specific indication (chlamydia, pertussis, prokinetic for feeding intolerance).

drugs.monitoring: RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Headache, dizziness, mild thrombocytopenia (rare); accumulation in renal impairment.
drugs.renal_adjustment: Reduce dose / extend interval if Cr clearance < 50 mL/min.

review.notes: Preferred H₂RA in NICU after ranitidine withdrawal (NDMA). PPIs are an alternative for severe GERD.

india_formulations.strength: 0.4 mg, 1 mg, 5 mg tablet; 0.4 mg/mL drops

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 56 mcg/kg/day (max 140 mcg/day for ≥ 3 kg ; 91 mcg/day for 1–3 kg ; 42 mcg/day for < 1 kg)

doses.indication: Maternal folate deficiency, breast-fed infant supplement

drugs.monitoring: Routine clinical; CBC if deficient.
drugs.adverse_effects: None at usual doses.

cross_checks.source: AAP — Iron / vitamin supplementation in preterm

drugs.monitoring: BP, HR (reflex tachycardia), urine output.
drugs.adverse_effects: Reflex tachycardia, hypotension, fluid retention; lupus-like syndrome (chronic).

review.notes: Often paired with a β-blocker to attenuate reflex tachycardia.

doses.comments: Distinct from chlorothiazide — do not confuse.

drugs.monitoring: Electrolytes (K, Na), urine output.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypokalaemia, hyponatraemia, hyperuricaemia, hypercalcaemia, hyperglycaemia.

review.notes: Often combined with spironolactone in BPD chronic care.

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, RFT, neuro status (seizure risk).
drugs.adverse_effects: Diarrhoea, nausea, transaminitis, seizures (relatively higher than meropenem — caution in renal impairment / CNS infection), eosinophilia.

review.notes: Meropenem generally preferred over imipenem in CNS infections — lower seizure risk.

doses.comments: Adjust by 10–20% based on Finnegan score. Standardised stepwise wean (8 steps over ~16 days).

drugs.monitoring: Finnegan / NAS score, RR, SpO₂, ECG (QT prolongation), feeding pattern.
drugs.adverse_effects: Respiratory depression, sedation, prolonged QT, NAS rebound on rapid wean.
drugs.contraindications: Severe respiratory depression without ventilation; concurrent serotonergic drugs (relative — serotonin syndrome).
drugs.black_box_warning: Respiratory depression, addiction, QT prolongation. Use only by experienced practitioners.

review.notes: Methadone has a long, variable half-life — close monitoring during titration. Morphine and buprenorphine are alternatives with shorter NICU stays.

doses.indication: Hypoxic respiratory failure with PPHN (term / late preterm)
doses.comments: If PaO₂ rises ≥ 60 torr within 4 h, decrease to 5 ppm. Wean FiO₂ first; when FiO₂ < 0.6, wean iNO by 1 ppm every ~4 h. Stop when stable on 1 ppm × 4 h.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous SpO₂, pre/post-ductal SpO₂, ABG, methaemoglobin level (every 12 h initially), NO₂ (gas analyser), echocardiogram.
drugs.adverse_effects: Methaemoglobinaemia, NO₂ toxicity (lung injury), rebound pulmonary hypertension on abrupt withdrawal, bleeding (platelet inhibition — minor).
drugs.contraindications: Right-to-left shunt-dependent congenital heart disease (e.g. severe coarctation, interrupted arch); severe LV dysfunction (relative — may worsen pulmonary oedema).

review.notes: Routine use in preterm < 34 weeks NOT recommended (no benefit, possible harm). Echo to rule out left heart obstruction is mandatory before starting.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_range: Titrate up by 1 mcg/kg/hr every 24 h ; max 10 mcg/kg/hr
doses.duration: Wean over 2–7 days when chyle drainage falls

drugs.monitoring: Glucose, GH, IGF-1 (chronic), thyroid, gallbladder USG (long-term), GI symptoms.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hyperglycaemia / hypoglycaemia, diarrhoea, bile-stone formation (chronic), necrotising enterocolitis (case reports in preterm), growth suppression (chronic).

review.notes: Preterm — caution due to possible NEC association.

doses.indication: Severe GERD / stress ulcer prophylaxis (refractory to H₂RA)
doses.comments: From PK studies in neonates — 2.5 mg orally daily provides similar exposure to 40 mg in adults.

drugs.monitoring: Watch for infection (NEC, pneumonia), Mg, B12 (chronic).
drugs.adverse_effects: Increased risk of Clostridioides difficile, NEC, pneumonia (with prolonged use). Hypomagnesaemia. Cobalamin / Fe deficiency (chronic).

review.notes: Long-term PPI use in preterm has been associated with NEC and late-onset sepsis — use shortest course possible.

doses.route: IV (over 1 h, peripheral max 40 mmol/L; central max 80 mmol/L)

drugs.administration: 1 g KCl = 13.4 mEq K⁺. 1 mEq K⁺ = 74.6 mg KCl. Always document mEq, not mg of salt.
drugs.monitoring: ECG (peaked T, QT shortening, arrhythmia), serum K⁺ every 4–6 h during correction.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hyperkalaemia (cardiac arrest), phlebitis, IV-site pain.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT push undiluted IV — fatal cardiac arrest.

review.notes: PediAid has a Potassium Correction calculator.

doses.indication: Infantile haemangioma (≥ 5 weeks of age, ≥ 2 kg)
doses.loading_dose_per_kg: Week 1: 0.6 mg/kg/dose BD ; Week 2: 1.1 mg/kg/dose BD ; Week 3 onwards: 1.7 mg/kg/dose BD
doses.frequency: twice daily, ≥ 9 hours apart, with feeds
doses.duration: 6 months (typical); continue until full involution / 1 yr of age

drugs.monitoring: HR, BP, glucose (hypoglycaemia in neonates), bronchospasm signs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Bradycardia, hypotension, hypoglycaemia (especially in fasting / illness), bronchospasm, sleep disturbance.
drugs.contraindications: Bradyarrhythmia / heart block (without pacemaker), severe asthma, cardiogenic shock, congenital heart block.

review.notes: Always feed with each propranolol dose to avoid hypoglycaemia. Hold on intercurrent illness with poor feeding.

doses.frequency: Single dose with EEG monitoring; if seizures stop within minutes, diagnostic

doses.comments: Higher doses may be required during intercurrent illness.

drugs.monitoring: Seizure response, neurodevelopmental progress.
drugs.adverse_effects: High-dose IV pyridoxine: rare apnoea, hypotonia. Chronic high-dose: peripheral neuropathy.

review.notes: Always trial in any neonatal refractory seizure NOT controlled by phenobarbital + phenytoin.

doses.indication: Adjunct for staph — endocarditis / prosthetic infection (with prosthetic material)
Haemophilus influenzae type b prophylaxis (close contacts)

drugs.monitoring: LFTs, CBC, drug interactions (potent CYP3A4 inducer — lowers levels of many drugs).
drugs.adverse_effects: Orange-red discolouration of secretions / urine (harmless), transaminitis, thrombocytopenia, rash.

cross_checks.source: AAP Red Book — Hib, meningococcal prophylaxis

review.notes: TB-specific dosing (10–20 mg/kg/day) is per IAP/RNTCP guidelines and not detailed here.

drugs.monitoring: Train-of-four, ventilator synchrony, eye and pressure-area care.
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachycardia (mild), prolonged paralysis with hepatic / renal impairment, anaphylaxis (rare but documented).
drugs.contraindications: Use only by trained clinicians familiar with airway management.

review.notes: Reverse with sugammadex 16 mg/kg (immediate) or 4 mg/kg (deep block). Vecuronium is alternative for prolonged paralysis.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_range: Titrate to BP, MAX 10 mcg/kg/min — but no longer than 10 min at max rate
doses.comments: Risk of cyanide / thiocyanate toxicity beyond 72 h or rates > 2 mcg/kg/min — switch to alternative if prolonged.

drugs.administration: Protect from light (light wrap on bag and tubing). Use central vein. Mix with D5W only.
drugs.monitoring: Continuous arterial BP, ECG; serum thiocyanate level (> 50 mg/L toxic) if used > 48 h or in renal impairment; serum cyanide / lactate (if metabolic acidosis develops).
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension (rapid onset), cyanide toxicity, thiocyanate accumulation (renal impairment), methaemoglobinaemia.
drugs.contraindications: Compensatory hypertension (e.g. coarctation, AV shunt); severe hepatic impairment; high-output heart failure.
drugs.renal_adjustment: If GFR < 30 mL/min/1.73 m²: limit mean rate < 3 mcg/kg/min. Anuria: limit < 1 mcg/kg/min.

review.notes: Co-administer hydroxocobalamin or sodium thiosulfate for cyanide protection if prolonged use.

doses.indication: Diuresis (BPD, CHF, ascites) — often combined with HCTZ

drugs.monitoring: Electrolytes (K — risk of hyperkalaemia), Na, urine output, BP, RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hyperkalaemia, gynaecomastia (chronic), GI upset.
drugs.contraindications: Anuria, hyperkalaemia, severe renal impairment, Addison disease.

review.notes: Often paired with thiazide (HCTZ) for BPD chronic diuresis — counterbalances K loss.

doses.indication: Tracheal intubation (rare in NICU — rocuronium preferred)
doses.frequency: Single immediately before intubation; max cumulative 4 mg/kg

drugs.monitoring: ECG, SpO₂, airway, K (especially in burns / muscular dystrophy / prolonged immobilisation — hyperkalaemia risk).
drugs.adverse_effects: Hyperkalaemia (especially burns, crush injury, muscular disease — risk of cardiac arrest), bradycardia (especially repeat doses), masseter spasm, malignant hyperthermia.
drugs.contraindications: Burn / crush injury > 24 h, paraplegia / muscular dystrophy, hyperkalaemia, malignant hyperthermia history. Many NICU experts AVOID due to bradycardia and HyperK risk in sick neonates.
drugs.black_box_warning: Risk of cardiac arrest from acute hyperkalaemia, especially in undiagnosed muscle / metabolic disease — rocuronium preferred for paediatric intubation.

review.notes: Rocuronium is preferred over succinylcholine for routine NICU intubation.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: PMA-based: ≤ 29 wk: 5 mg/kg q48h (0–7 d), 4 mg/kg q36h (8–28 d), q24h (≥ 29 d) ; 30–34 wk: 4.5 mg/kg q36h (0–7 d), 4 mg/kg q24h (≥ 8 d) ; ≥ 35 wk: 4 mg/kg q24h

drugs.monitoring: Therapeutic levels — Peak (30 min post): 5–12 mg/L (target 8–13 for extended-interval) ; Trough: < 1 mg/L. Audiology screen.
drugs.adverse_effects: Same as gentamicin — nephrotoxic, oto- and vestibular toxic.
drugs.black_box_warning: Aminoglycoside class — neurotoxicity, ototoxicity, nephrotoxicity. Risk increased with renal impairment, dehydration, prolonged therapy.

review.notes: Tobramycin is preferred over gentamicin against Pseudomonas (slightly lower MICs).
